data.body:
## Context
AI agents exploring the DSO codebase (during planning, investigation, and implementation) frequently miss cross-file dependencies because they rely on text-based grep, which cannot distinguish structural code patterns from comments or string literals. This leads to incomplete impact analysis during planning — the dominant contributor to cross-cutting interaction bugs (17 fix commits in the last month). ast-grep provides structural code search across 170+ languages including bash, enabling agents to find function definitions, callers, source-chain dependencies, and pattern usage with syntax awareness. ast-grep complements MCP-based semantic search (dso-soyt): ast-grep is deterministic structural pattern matching; semantic search is conceptual similarity retrieval.

## Success Criteria
- ast-grep is documented as a recommended dependency: installation instructions (brew install ast-grep on macOS, cargo install ast-grep --locked on Linux) in README and /dso:project-setup
- CLAUDE.md references sg as the preferred tool for structural code search when exploring cross-file dependencies, with specific example patterns
- Skills reference ast-grep in specific dependency discovery steps: implementation-plan Step 3 (file impact table), fix-bug Step 2 (investigation pre-loading), sprint Phase 3 (batch overlap context), brainstorm Phase 2 Step 2.5 (technical self-review). Each includes a command -v sg guard with Grep fallback.
- Graceful degradation: skills check command -v sg before use and fall back to Grep tool when ast-grep is not installed
- Agents demonstrably use sg commands during exploration — verified by reviewing session transcripts after rollout

## Dependencies
None. Complements dso-soyt (MCP semantic search).

## Approach
Tool-only integration: install ast-grep as a documented dependency, add CLAUDE.md references and skill guidance for agents to use sg commands during exploration. No wrapper scripts or deep integration into gates — purely agent-facing guidance with graceful fallback.
